public List<Run> getRuns(String s) {
    List<Run> runs = new ArrayList<>();
    int n = s.length();
    if (n == 0) return runs;
    char current = s.charAt(0);
    int count = 1;
    for (int i=1; i < n; i++) {
        if (s.charAt(i) == current) {
            count++;
        } else {
            runs.add(new Run(current, count));
            current = s.charAt(i);
            count = 1;
        }
    }
    runs.add(new Run(current, count));
    return runs;
}

public int maximumLength(String s) {
    int n = s.length();
    int[][] counts = new int[26][n+2]; // indexes 0-25 for a-z, k up to n.

    // process runs
    int i=0;
    while (i < n) {
        char currentChar = s.charAt(i);
        int j = i;
        while (j < n && s.charAt(j) == currentChar) {
            j++;
        }
        int runLength = j - i; // length of the run
        // process this run
        for (int k=1; k <= runLength; k++) {
            int contribution = runLength - k + 1;
            int cIndex = currentChar - 'a';
            counts[cIndex][k] += contribution;
        }
        i = j;
    }

    // Now check for each character and each possible k
    int maxK = -1;
    for (int c=0; c < 26; c++) {
        for (int k=n; k >=1; k--) {
            if (counts[c][k] >=3) {
                if (k > maxK) {
                    maxK = k;
                }
                break; // since we are checking from largest k down
            }
        }
    }

    return maxK == -1 ? -1 : maxK;
}

public int maximumLength(String s) {
    int n = s.length();
    int[][] counts = new int[26][n + 2]; // indexes 0-25 for a-z, k up to n.

    int i = 0;
    while (i < n) {
        char currentChar = s.charAt(i);
        int j = i;
        while (j < n && s.charAt(j) == currentChar) {
            j++;
        }
        int runLength = j - i;
        // process this run
        for (int k = 1; k <= runLength; k++) {
            int contribution = runLength - k + 1;
            int cIndex = currentChar - 'a';
            counts[cIndex][k] += contribution;
        }
        i = j;
    }

    int maxK = -1;
    for (int c = 0; c < 26; c++) {
        for (int k = n; k >= 1; k--) {
            if (counts[c][k] >= 3) {
                if (k > maxK) {
                    maxK = k;
                }
                break;
            }
        }
    }
    return maxK == -1 ? -1 : maxK;
}

class Solution {
    public int maximumLength(String s) {
        int n = s.length();
        int[][] counts = new int[26][n + 2]; // indexes 0-25 for a-z, k up to n.

        int i = 0;
        while (i < n) {
            char currentChar = s.charAt(i);
            int j = i;
            while (j < n && s.charAt(j) == currentChar) {
                j++;
            }
            int runLength = j - i;
            // process this run
            for (int k = 1; k <= runLength; k++) {
                int contribution = runLength - k + 1;
                int cIndex = currentChar - 'a';
                counts[cIndex][k] += contribution;
            }
            i = j;
        }

        int maxK = -1;
        for (int c = 0; c < 26; c++) {
            for (int k = n; k >= 1; k--) {
                if (counts[c][k] >= 3) {
                    if (k > maxK) {
                        maxK = k;
                    }
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
        return maxK == -1 ? -1 : maxK;
    }
}

But wait, in the code, for each run, the contribution is added for all k from 1 to runLength. But for example, for a run of length 5, the code adds contribution for k=1, 2, 3, 4, 5. Which is correct.

solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int maximumLength(String s) {
        int n = s.length();
        int[][] counts = new int[26][n + 2]; 

        int i = 0;
        while (i < n) {
            char currentChar = s.charAt(i);
            int j = i;
            while (j < n && s.charAt(j) == currentChar) {
                j++;
            }
            int runLength = j - i;
            for (int k = 1; k <= runLength; k++) {
                int contribution = runLength - k + 1;
                int cIndex = currentChar - 'a';
                counts[cIndex][k] += contribution;
            }
            i = j;
        }

        int maxK = -1;
        for (int c = 0; c < 26; c++) {
            for (int k = n; k >= 1; k--) {
                if (counts[c][k] >= 3) {
                    if (k > maxK) {
                        maxK = k;
                    }
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
        return maxK == -1 ? -1 : maxK;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n^2)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution processes the input string to identify runs of consecutive characters. For each run, it calculates how many special substrings of each possible length can be formed. These counts are aggregated per character and length. The algorithm then checks for the maximum length where any character has at least three occurrences. Strengths include efficient handling of small input sizes (n ≤ 50) and clarity. Weaknesses involve O(n²) time complexity, which may not scale well for larger inputs.
